findings.message: The RUM query-param allowlist (frontend_rum_allowed_query_params) captures attribution params into telemetry. Adding "email" collects the user's email address from the query string into telemetry/RUM data. This is PII collection not needed for the stated purpose (UTM campaign attribution), causing PII to be ingested and likely retained in observability/telemetry stores.
findings.suggestion: Remove "email" from the allowlist. Keep the list restricted to non-PII attribution params (utm_*). If a user identifier is genuinely required, use a pseudonymous/hashed token rather than a raw email, and document the purpose and retention.
